title: Protocol for Evaluating In Vivo the Activation of the P2RX7 Immunomodulator
author: Janho dit Hreich, Juhel, et al.
description: BackgroundP2RX7 is a purinergic receptor with pleiotropic activities that is activated by high levels of extracellular ATP that are found in inflamed tissues. P2RX7 has immunomodulatory and anti-tumor proprieties and is therefore a therapeutic target for various diseases. Several compounds are developed to either inhibit or enhance its activation. However, studying their effect on P2RX7’s activities is limited to in vitro and ex vivo studies that require the use of unphysiological media that could affect its activation. Up to now, the only way to assess the activity of P2RX7 modulators on the receptor in vivo was in an indirect manner.ResultsWe successfully developed a protocol allowing the detection of P2RX7 activation in vivo in lungs of mice, by taking advantage of its unique macropore formation ability. The protocol is based on intranasal delivery of TO-PRO™-3, a non-permeant DNA intercalating dye, and fluorescence measurement by flow cytometry. We show that ATP enhances TO-PRO™-3 fluorescence mainly in lung immune cells of mice in a P2RX7-dependant manner.ConclusionsThe described approach has allowed the successful analysis of P2RX7 activity directly in the lungs of WT and transgenic C57BL6 mice. The provided detailed guidelines and recommendations will support the use of this protocol to study the potency of pharmacologic or biologic compounds targeting P2RX7.Supplementary InformationThe online version contains supplementary material available at 10.1186/s12575-022-00188-6.

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: The described methods include the description of the work, along with general guidelines that can be used for implementing the strategy with various compounds described to modulate P2RX7 activity.

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Anesthetize mouse i.p. with a mixture of 25 mg/kg ketamine and 2.5 mg/kg xylazine using 1 ml syringe with a 26G needle.

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Place mouse in a box with 5% isoflurane for 1 minute.

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: When asleep, mouse is held upright and was given 25 μl of TO-PRO™-3 (1 μM/kg) in one nostril then 25 μl of ATP (100 mM) or 1X PBS in another using a micropipette.

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Sacrifice mouse by cervical elongation

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Spray mouse thorax with 70% ethanol. Harvest lungs using fine and sharp scissors and pliers.

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Separate the lobes. Transfer the lobes into a GentleMACS™ C-tube (Miltenyi Biotech) containing the enzymatic buffer for dissociation (Lung dissociation kit, Miltenyi Biotech)

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Dissociate lungs using the 37_m_LDK1 program on a GentleMACS™ Octo dissociator with heaters (Miltenyi Biotech)

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: At the end of the program, resuspend cells and pass them through a 100 μm cell strainer on top of a 50 ml tube for single cell suspensions. Wash the GentleMACS™ C-tube with 1X Buffer S (Lung dissociation kit, Miltenyi Biotech) for maximum retrieval of cells and pass them though the 100 μm cell strainer.

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Discard the cell strainer and centrifuge the tube at 1200 rpm 4 °C for 5 minutes.

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Aspirate completely the supernatant and resuspend pellet gently with 1 ml of ice-cold ACK lysis buffer (Gibco) for 30 seconds to lyse red blood cells.

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Add 30 ml of 1X PBS for stopping the reaction.

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Centrifuge at 1200 rpm 4 °C for 5 minutes

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Count cells

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Add 1 million of lung single cells in 150 μl 1X PBS in a 5 ml polypropylene tube

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Add 150 μl of Green Live/Dead stain (2X) (Invitrogen) or 1X PBS (unstained control) into the 5 ml polypropylene tube

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Transfer cell suspension to a 96 well V plate

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Centrifuge plate at 1300 rpm 4 °C for 3 minutes

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Flick the plate

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Block Fc receptors using anti-CD16/32 (dil 1/100, BD Biosciences) diluted in FACS Buffer (1X PBS, 5% FBS, 0.5% EDTA) in the dark under agitation for 10 minutes

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Centrifuge plate at 1300 rpm 4 °C for 3 minutes

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Flick plate

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Add antibody mix diluted in FACS buffer and homogenize immediately using a multichannel pipette.Antibodies: CD45 BUV395 (dil 1/100, BD Biosciences), P2RX7 PE (dil 1/8, Biolegend) or isotype control rat IgG2b κ PE (dil 1/8, Biolegend).

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Cells were acquired using the CytoFLEX LX (Beckman Coulter)

steps_raw.title: Methods
steps_raw.instruction: Samples were analyzed using FlowJo (LLC)

Add 2.4 ml of 1X Buffer S, 15 μl of enzyme A and 100 μl of enzyme D per GentleMACS™ C-tube (Miltenyi Biotech)

steps_raw.instruction:

steps_raw.instruction: Dilute LIVE/DEAD™ in 1X PBS to 2X working concentration per tube. Add 0.4 μL LIVE/DEAD™ in 150 μL per tube.

steps_raw.instruction:
